heading: Art. 48. Contempt
label: 848 Art. 48. Contempt
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)84850:623.(Art. 48), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 123The word “may” is substituted for the word “shall”.

Amendments2017—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “ (article 66(h))” for “ (article 66(g))”.Pub. L. 115–91section 866(h) of this titlesection 866(g) of this title2016—, amended section catchline generally, substituting “Contempt” for “Contempts”.Pub. L. 114–328, § 5230(c)Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “A judge detailed to a court-martial, a court of inquiry, the United States Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces, a military Court of Criminal Appeals, a provost court, or a military commission may punish for contempt any person who—Pub. L. 114–328, § 5230(a)“(1) uses any menacing word, sign, or gesture in the presence of the judge during the proceedings of the court-martial, court, or military commission;“(2) disturbs the proceedings of the court-martial, court, or military commission by any riot or disorder; or“(3) willfully disobeys the lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command of the court-martial, court, or military commission.”Subsecs. (c), (d). , added subsec. (c) and redesignated former subsec. (c) as (d).Pub. L. 114–328, § 5230(b)2011— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “A court-martial, provost court, or military commission may punish for contempt any person who uses any menacing word, sign, or gesture in its presence, or who disturbs its proceedings by any riot or disorder. The punishment may not exceed confinement for 30 days or a fine of $100, or both. This section does not apply to a military commission established under chapter 47A of this title.”Pub. L. 111–3832006— inserted last sentence.Pub. L. 109–366

children.label: A
children.text: uses any menacing word, sign, or gesture in the presence of the judicial officer during the proceeding;

children.label: B
children.text: disturbs the proceeding by any riot or disorder; or

children.label: C
children.text: willfully disobeys a lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command issued with respect to the proceeding.

children.label: A
children.text: Any judge of the Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces and any judge of a Court of Criminal Appeals under (article 66).section 866 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Any military judge detailed to a court-martial, a provost court, a military commission, or any other proceeding under this chapter.

children.label: C
children.text: Any military magistrate designated to preside under (article 19).section 819 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: The president of a court of inquiry.

children.heading: .—Punishment
children.label: b .—Punishment
children.text: The punishment for contempt under subsection (a) may not exceed confinement for 30 days, a fine of $1,000, or both.

if imposed by a military judge or military magistrate, may be reviewed by the Court of Criminal Appeals in accordance with the uniform rules of procedure for the Courts of Criminal Appeals under (article 66(h));section 866(h) of this title

if imposed by a judge of the Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces or a judge of a Court of Criminal Appeals, shall constitute a judgment of the court, subject to review under the applicable provisions of section 867 or 867a of this title (article 67 or 67a); and
if imposed by a court of inquiry, shall be subject to review by the convening authority in accordance with rules prescribed by the President.

children.heading: 47.—Inapplicability to Military Commissions Under ChapterA
children.label: d 47.—Inapplicability to Military Commissions Under ChapterA
children.text: This section does not apply to a military commission established under chapter 47A of this title.
